summary_one_line: Winner inputs encode the HTTP/2 protocol-version literal so conn->httpversion reaches >=20, satisfying the PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP && httpversion>=20 gate.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Bytes that drive conn->httpversion to a value >= 20 (HTTP/2) while keeping PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP set — concretely the literal HTTP-version token 'HTTP/2' present in the seed (offsets ~0x24-0x2a: 47 48 54 54 50 2f 32 = 'GHTTP/2').
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: Every winner-resolving seed carries the literal 'HTTP/2' bytes (offset 0x24 G=47 concentrated x16, then HTTP/2 at 0x25-0x2a), and the per-role overlay shows the winner taking the true branch at line 470 (W T=1010) with httpversion==20 detected at readwrite_data L540.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds carry no HTTP/2 version token at those offsets (e.g. offset 0x24 is P/B/junk, not the concentrated G; HTTP-version field absent), so conn->httpversion never reaches 20 and the true side at line 470 is never taken (L T=20 only on the false-direction row, never resolving the blocked true side).
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: cmplog's I2SRandReplace reads the post-execution CmpLogObserver buffer for the httpversion / version-string comparison and substitutes the matching operand (the HTTP/2 version literal) directly into the input at the right offset; naive/value_profile have no I2SRandReplace and must stumble onto the exact multi-byte version token by blind havoc, which they fail to do in 10 trials. value_profile_cmplog wins over value_profile for the same I2S reason.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked branch is the HTTP/2 gate requiring conn->httpversion >= 20 under PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP.
Both decisive pairs are I2S deltas and the winners resolve while losers block.
The second decisive pair is also an I2S delta (value_profile_cmplog over value_profile).
Winner-resolving seeds contain the literal HTTP/2 version token.
The HTTP-version token byte 'G' is concentrated at offset 0x24 in winners but not losers.
The winner takes the true (blocked) side of line 470 while the loser does not resolve it.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L 470  T=1010 F=0  T=20 F=0  ((conn->handler->protocol&PROTO_FAMILY_HTTP) && conn->htt...  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: The HTTP/2 detection (httpversion==20) fires in the winner at readwrite_data L540 but not the loser.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=2   L 540  T=1010 F=12  T=0 F=20  (conn->httpversion == 20));

evidence_trail.claim: cmplog adds I2SRandReplace which substitutes a CMP operand at matching input offsets; naive has no I2S.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
`I2SRandReplace` reads the post-execution `CmpLogObserver` buffer,
picks a CMP entry, scans the input for byte sequences matching one
operand, and substitutes the other operand at those offsets.

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace, so the second pair's win is also attributable to I2S.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 415 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 1 --seed 32d10b449c4075ee` and observed 2 I2S-floor dash rows (mutation_op = -) at depths 23 and 24 of the 25-level ancestor chain, the direct lower-bound signal of an I2SRandReplace find under the current build.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness whose HTTP/2 version-establishing comparison is gated on a value never reached via operand substitution (e.g. computed, not literal-compared) on which cmplog still resolves the >=20 branch as fast as naive — showing the win was not from I2S literal substitution of the version token.

weakest_evidence_point: The exact CMP whose operand I2S substitutes (httpversion integer vs the 'HTTP/2' string parse) is inferred from source plus the concentrated version-token bytes, not directly observed in a CmpLog trace.
